codeReview.summary: The fix correctly addresses the scope-based validation issue. Changed validation from requiring 'read' operation specifically to checking that scope has at least one allowed operation. This allows git scopes (push/pull/fetch), LLM scopes (chat/embed), and API scopes (read/write) to all work correctly.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker identified and fixed the bug at line 470 (not 370 as described in the feature). The feature description may have been slightly inaccurate about the line number, but this doesn't affect the quality of the fix.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Feature description says 'line 370' but the actual check is at line 470 in credential_proxy.rs

sharedStateObservations.observation: Pre-existing formatting issues in gvisor.rs were flagged by cargo fmt --check but are unrelated to this fix. Not a shared state gap but worth noting.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows: 'Format check shows pre-existing formatting issues in gvisor.rs (not related to my fix)'

summary: Review passed. The fix correctly changes the validation check from requiring 'read' operation to checking that scope has at least one allowed operation. This resolves the issue where git scopes (push/pull/fetch) and LLM scopes (chat/embed) were being rejected. All 602 workspace tests pass, including all 20 credential proxy tests. Worker followed proper Rust skill procedures.
